terms.definition: The systemic capacity for social groups to maintain internal alignment of values, norms, and practices over time.
A point in a dynamical system where one state trajectory splits into two qualitatively different paths.
The mean annual growth rate of an investment or population over a specified time period longer than one year.
The period required for a quantity growing exponentially to double in size.
The average number of children that would be born to a woman over her lifetime given current age-specific fertility rates.
A graphical illustration that shows the distribution of various age groups in a population.
The locally negotiated, orally transmitted code of allowable technologies and behaviors within an Amish church district.
A culturally sanctioned period of semi-autonomy during Amish adolescence, preceding adult church membership decisions.
The proposition that religious groups requiring higher sacrifices from members achieve higher commitment and retention.
A geographically contiguous cluster of one or more Amish church districts operating under shared leadership.
The monetary value of all finished goods and services produced within a specific sub-state region in a given period.
Economic activity generated by visitors traveling to agricultural areas for leisure, education, or retail experiences.
